description: Reading docker/claude-code-job/Dockerfile

content.content:
1→FROM node:22-bookworm-slim
     2→
     3→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
     4→    git \
     5→    jq \
     6→    curl \
     7→    procps \
     8→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
     9→
    10→# Install GitHub CLI
    11→RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    12→    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null \
    13→    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y gh \
    14→    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
    15→
    16→RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
    17→
    18→# Claude Code refuses --dangerously-skip-permissions as root.
    19→# Create a non-root user and give it ownership of /job.
    20→RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent \
    21→    && mkdir -p /job \
    22→    && chown agent:agent /job
    23→
    24→COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
    25→RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh
    26→
    27→# Install Playwright Chromium + system deps at build time (used by Playwright MCP)
    28→# PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH puts browsers in a shared location so the non-root
    29→# `agent` user can find what root installed during build.
    30→ENV PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/opt/pw-browsers
    31→RUN npx -y playwright install --with-deps chromium \
    32→    && chown -R agent:agent /opt/pw-browsers
    33→
    34→USER agent
    35→WORKDIR /job
    36→
    37→ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]
    38→

description: Reading .github/workflows/run-job.yml

content.content:
1→name: Agent Job
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  create:
     5→
     6→jobs:
     7→  run-agent:
     8→    runs-on: ${{ vars.RUNS_ON || 'ubuntu-latest' }}
     9→    if: github.ref_type == 'branch' && startsWith(github.ref_name, 'job/')
    10→    permissions:
    11→      contents: write
    12→      packages: read
    13→
    14→    steps:
    15→      - name: Extract job ID
    16→        id: job
    17→        run: |
    18→          JOB_ID="${{ github.ref_name }}"
    19→          echo "id=${JOB_ID#job/}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    20→
    21→      - name: Checkout
    22→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    23→        with:
    24→          ref: ${{ github.ref_name }}
    25→          sparse-checkout: |
    26→            package-lock.json
    27→            logs/*/job.config.json
    28→          sparse-checkout-cone-mode: false
    29→
    30→      - name: Get thepopebot version
    31→        id: version
    32→        run: |
    33→          if [ -f package-lock.json ]; then
    34→            VERSION=$(jq -r '.packages["node_modules/thepopebot"].version // empty' package-lock.json)
    35→          fi
    36→          VERSION="${VERSION:-${{ vars.THEPOPEBOT_VERSION || '1.2.74-beta.30' }}}"
    37→          echo "tag=$VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    38→
    39→      - name: Read job config overrides
    40→        id: job-config
    41→        run: |
    42→          CONFIG_FILE="logs/${{ steps.job.outputs.id }}/job.config.json"
    43→          if [ -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    44→            echo "llm_provider=$(jq -r '.llm_provider // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    45→            echo "llm_model=$(jq -r '.llm_model // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    46→            echo "agent_backend=$(jq -r '.agent_backend // empty' "$CONFIG_FILE")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    47→          fi
    48→
    49→      - name: Login to GHCR
    50→        if: startsWith(vars.JOB_IMAGE_URL, 'ghcr.io/')
    51→        uses: docker/login-action@v3
    52→        with:
    53→          registry: ghcr.io
    54→          username: ${{ github.actor }}
    55→          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    56→
    57→      - name: Fetch secrets from Doppler
    58→        id: doppler
    59→        env:
    60→          DOPPLER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOPPLER_TOKEN }}
    61→        run: |
    62→          # Fetch all secrets from Doppler at runtime — no keys stored in GitHub
    63→          DOPPLER_SECRETS=$(curl -sf \
    64→            "https://api.doppler.com/v3/configs/config/secrets/download?project=bigman-engine&config=prd&format=json" \
    65→            -H "Authorization: Bearer $DOPPLER_TOKEN")
    66→
    67→          if [ -z "$DOPPLER_SECRETS" ]; then
    68→            echo "::error::Failed to fetch secrets from Doppler"
    69→            exit 1
    70→          fi
    71→
    72→          # Build SECRETS JSON for the job container (PopeBot entrypoint parses this)
    73→          SECRETS=$(echo "$DOPPLER_SECRETS" | jq -c '{
    74→            ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: .ANTHROPIC_API_KEY,
    75→            BRAVE_API_KEY: .BRAVE_API_KEY,
    76→            GH_TOKEN: .GH_TOKEN,
    77→            OBSIDIAN_API_KEY: .OBSIDIAN_API_KEY,
    78→            OBSIDIAN_HOST: .OBSIDIAN_HOST,
    79→            WEBHOOK_SECRET: .WEBHOOK_SECRET
    80→          }')
    81→          echo "SECRETS<<EOSECRETS" >> $GITHUB_ENV
    82→          echo "$SECRETS" >> $GITHUB_ENV
    83→          echo "EOSECRETS" >> $GITHUB_ENV
    84→
    85→          # Export individual env vars — single jq pass for masking + export
    86→          echo "$DOPPLER_SECRETS" | jq -r '
    87→            to_entries[]
    88→            | select(.key | test("^DOPPLER_") | not)
    89→            | @text "\(.key)\t\(.value)"
    90→          ' | while IFS=$'\t' read -r key val; do
    91→            # Mask actual secrets (keys/tokens >20 chars), not config values
    92→            if echo "$key" | grep -qiE 'KEY|SECRET|TOKEN' && [ ${#val} -gt 20 ]; then
    93→              echo "::add-mask::$val"
    94→            fi
    95→            echo "$key<<EOF_$key" >> $GITHUB_ENV
    96→            echo "$val" >> $GITHUB_ENV
    97→            echo "EOF_$key" >> $GITHUB_ENV
    98→          done
    99→
   100→      - name: Run thepopebot Agent
   101→        env:
   102→          JOB_IMAGE_URL: ${{ vars.JOB_IMAGE_URL }}
   103→          THEPOPEBOT_VERSION: ${{ steps.version.outputs.tag }}
   104→          LLM_MODEL: ${{ steps.job-config.outputs.llm_model || vars.LLM_MODEL }}
   105→          LLM_PROVIDER: ${{ steps.job-config.outputs.llm_provider || vars.LLM_PROVIDER }}
   106→          OPENAI_BASE_URL: ${{ vars.OPENAI_BASE_URL }}
   107→          AGENT_BACKEND: ${{ steps.job-config.outputs.agent_backend || vars.AGENT_BACKEND || '' }}
   108→        run: |
   109→          AGENT_BACKEND="${AGENT_BACKEND:-pi}"
   110→          if [ -n "$JOB_IMAGE_URL" ]; then
   111→            IMAGE="${JOB_IMAGE_URL}:${THEPOPEBOT_VERSION}"
   112→          elif [ "$AGENT_BACKEND" = "claude-code" ]; then
   113→            IMAGE="stephengpope/thepopebot:claude-code-job-${THEPOPEBOT_VERSION}"
   114→          else
   115→            IMAGE="stephengpope/thepopebot:pi-coding-agent-job-${THEPOPEBOT_VERSION}"
   116→          fi
   117→          echo "Using image: $IMAGE"
   118→
   119→          export LLM_SECRETS='{}'
   120→
   121→          # The stock entrypoint unsets ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (designed for OAuth).
   122→          # We use API key auth, so patch the entrypoint to keep it.
   123→          docker run --rm \
   124→            --entrypoint /bin/bash \
   125→            -e REPO_URL="${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}.git" \
   126→            -e BRANCH="${{ github.ref_name }}" \
   127→            -e SECRETS \
   128→            -e LLM_SECRETS \
   129→            -e LLM_MODEL="${LLM_MODEL}" \
   130→            -e LLM_PROVIDER="${LLM_PROVIDER}" \
   131→            -e OPENAI_BASE_URL="${OPENAI_BASE_URL}" \
   132→            -e AGENT_BACKEND="${AGENT_BACKEND}" \
   133→            -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}" \
   134→            -e BRAVE_API_KEY="${BRAVE_API_KEY}" \
   135→            -e OBSIDIAN_API_KEY="${OBSIDIAN_API_KEY}" \
   136→            -e OBSIDIAN_HOST="${OBSIDIAN_HOST}" \
   137→            "$IMAGE" \
   138→            -c 'cp /entrypoint.sh /tmp/entrypoint.sh && sed -i "/unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/d" /tmp/entrypoint.sh && exec /tmp/entrypoint.sh'
   139→
   140→      - name: Sync outputs to Obsidian vault
   141→        if: always()
   142→        run: |
   143→          # Find the commit with job outputs (before the cleanup commit)
   144→          LOG_SHA=$(curl -sf \
   145→            "https://api.github.com/repos/${{ github.repository }}/commits?sha=${{ github.ref_name }}&per_page=3" \
   146→            -H "Authorization: token ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}" | \
   147→            jq -r '[.[] | select(.commit.message | test("Agent Job"))] | .[0].sha // empty')
   148→
   149→          if [ -z "$LOG_SHA" ]; then
   150→            echo "No job output commit found, skipping vault sync"
   151→            exit 0
   152→          fi
   153→
   154→          echo "Syncing job ${{ steps.job.outputs.id }} outputs to Obsidian vault..."
   155→
   156→          # Use WEBHOOK_SECRET directly (already exported as individual env var)
   157→          curl -s --connect-timeout 10 -X POST \
   158→            "http://${{ vars.PUBLIC_HOST }}:${{ vars.VAULT_SYNC_PORT || '3005' }}/vault-sync" \
   159→            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   160→            -H "x-webhook-secret: $WEBHOOK_SECRET" \
   161→            -d "{\"job_id\": \"${{ steps.job.outputs.id }}\", \"commit_sha\": \"$LOG_SHA\"}"
   162→

    12→# Export SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars (GH_TOKEN, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, etc.)

    16→
    17→# Export LLM_SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars
    18→if [ -n "$LLM_SECRETS" ]; then

    20→fi
    21→
    22→# Unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY so Claude Code uses the OAuth token.
    23→# If both are set, Claude Code prioritizes API key (billing to API credits)
    24→# which defeats the purpose. The API key is for the event handler, not here.
    25→unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
    26→
    27→# Git setup - derive identity from GitHub token
    28→gh auth setup-git
    29→GH_USER_JSON=$(gh api user -q '{name: .name, login: .login, email: .email, id: .id}')
    30→GH_USER_NAME=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.name // .login')
    31→GH_USER_EMAIL=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.email // "\(.id)+\(.login)@users.noreply.github.com"')
    32→git config --global user.name "$GH_USER_NAME"
    33→git config --global user.email "$GH_USER_EMAIL"
    34→
    35→# Clone branch
    36→if [ -n "$REPO_URL" ]; then
    37→    git clone --single-branch --branch "$BRANCH" --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" /job
    38→else
    39→    echo "No REPO_URL provided"
    40→fi
    41→
    42→cd /job
    43→
    44→WORKSPACE_DIR=$(pwd)
    45→
    46→# Skip onboarding and trust dialogs
    47→mkdir -p ~/.claude
    48→cat > ~/.claude/settings.json << 'EOF'
    49→{
    50→  "theme": "dark",
    51→  "hasTrustDialogAccepted": true,
    52→  "skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt": true
    53→}
    54→EOF
    55→
    56→cat > ~/.claude.json << ENDJSON
    57→{
    58→  "hasCompletedOnboarding": true,
    59→  "projects": {
    60→    "${WORKSPACE_DIR}": {
    61→      "allowedTools": ["WebSearch"],
    62→      "hasTrustDialogAccepted": true,
    63→      "hasTrustDialogHooksAccepted": true
    64→    }
    65→  }
    66→}
    67→ENDJSON
    68→
    69→# Install npm deps for active skills (native deps need correct Linux arch)
    70→for skill_dir in /job/skills/active/*/; do
    71→    if [ -f "${skill_dir}package.json" ]; then

    73→        (cd "$skill_dir" && npm install --omit=dev --no-package-lock)
    74→    fi
    75→done
    76→
    77→# Register Playwright MCP server (browser automation via native Claude Code tools)
    78→claude mcp add --transport stdio playwright -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest --headless --browser chromium
    79→
    80→# Setup logs
    81→LOG_DIR="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}"
    82→mkdir -p "${LOG_DIR}"
    83→
    84→# Build system prompt from config MD files
    85→SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/system-prompt.md"
    86→SYSTEM_FILES=("SOUL.md" "JOB_AGENT.md")
    87→> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
    88→for i in "${!SYSTEM_FILES[@]}"; do
    89→    cat "/job/config/${SYSTEM_FILES[$i]}" >> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
    90→    if [ "$i" -lt $((${#SYSTEM_FILES[@]} - 1)) ]; then
    91→        echo -e "\n\n" >> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
    92→    fi
    93→done
    94→
    95→# Resolve {{datetime}} variable in system prompt
    96→sed -i "s/{{datetime}}/$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")/g" "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
    97→
    98→# Read job metadata from job.config.json
    99→JOB_CONFIG="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}/job.config.json"
   100→TITLE=$(jq -r '.title // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")
   101→JOB_DESCRIPTION=$(jq -r '.job // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")
   102→
   103→PROMPT="
   104→
   105→# Your Job
   106→
   107→${JOB_DESCRIPTION}"
   108→
   109→# Build --model flag if LLM_MODEL is set
   110→MODEL_FLAG=""
   111→if [ -n "$LLM_MODEL" ]; then
   112→    MODEL_FLAG="--model $LLM_MODEL"
   113→fi
   114→
   115→# Run Claude Code — capture exit code instead of letting set -e kill the script
   116→# stream-json gives the full conversation trace (thinking, tool calls, results)
   117→# similar to Pi's .jsonl session logs
   118→set +e
   119→claude -p "$PROMPT" \
   120→    $MODEL_FLAG \
   121→    --append-system-prompt-file "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE" \
   122→    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
   123→    --verbose \
   124→    --output-format stream-json \
   125→    > "${LOG_DIR}/claude-session.jsonl" 2>"${LOG_DIR}/claude-stderr.log"
   126→AGENT_EXIT=$?
   127→
   128→# Commit based on outcome
   129→if [ $AGENT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
   130→    # Claude Code failed — only commit session logs, not partial code changes
   131→    git reset || true
   132→    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
   133→    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE} (failed)" || true
   134→else
   135→    # Claude Code succeeded — commit everything
   136→    git add -A
   137→    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
   138→    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" || true
   139→fi
   140→
   141→git push origin
   142→
   143→# Capture log commit SHA, then remove logs so they don't merge into main
   144→LOG_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
   145→git rm -rf "${LOG_DIR}"
   146→git commit -m "done." || true
   147→git push origin
   148→set -e
   149→
   150→# Create PR with log permalink (auto-merge handled by GitHub Actions workflow)
   151→REPO_SLUG=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner)
   152→LOG_URL="https://github.com/${REPO_SLUG}/tree/${LOG_SHA}/logs/${JOB_ID}"
   153→gh pr create --title "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" \
   154→  --body "📋 [View Job Logs](${LOG_URL})"$'\n\n---\n\n'"${JOB_DESCRIPTION}" \
   155→  --base main || true
   156→
   157→# Re-raise failure so the workflow reports it
   158→if [ $AGENT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
   159→    echo "Claude Code exited with code ${AGENT_EXIT}"
   160→    exit $AGENT_EXIT
   161→fi
   162→
   163→echo "Done. Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"
   164→

```bash

description: Reading skills/active/opportunity-analyser/stages/image_verify.py

content.content:
1→"""Image & listing verification stage.
     2→
     3→Runs post-scoring on Buy/Review products. Uses Claude Vision (Haiku) for:
     4→- Age-restriction warning detection
     5→- Counterfeit indicators
     6→- Listing mismatch detection
     7→- Pack size identification from images
     8→
     9→Also detects pack sizes from product titles.
    10→Does NOT change Buy/Review/Skip classification — adds image_flags and pack_size.
    11→"""
    12→import base64
    13→import json
    14→import os
    15→import re
    16→from datetime import datetime
    17→
    18→import anthropic
    19→import requests as http_requests
    20→
    21→import config
    22→from vault import log, strip_json_fences
    23→
    24→SKILL = "opportunity-analyser"
    25→
    26→VISION_PROMPT = """Analyse this product image for an e-commerce compliance check.
    27→
    28→Product: {product_name}
    29→EAN: {ean}
    30→
    31→Check for:
    32→1. AGE RESTRICTION: Does the packaging show age-restriction warnings (18+, alcohol, etc.)?
    33→2. COUNTERFEIT: Are there signs of counterfeit/fake product (misaligned labels, wrong fonts, low quality)?
    34→3. LISTING MISMATCH: Does the image match the product name and EAN description?
    35→4. CONDITION: Is the packaging damaged, opened, or not "new" condition?
    36→5. PACK SIZE: How many individual units are shown in the image? (1 = single, >1 = multipack)
    37→
    38→Return ONLY a JSON object:
    39→{{
    40→  "pass": true/false,
    41→  "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
    42→  "flags": ["list", "of", "issues"],
    43→  "pack_size_detected": number,
    44→  "notes": "brief explanation"
    45→}}"""
    46→
    47→
    48→
    49→
    50→def detect_pack_size(title):
    51→    """Detect pack size from product title string."""
    52→    if not title:
    53→        return 1
    54→    title_lower = title.lower()
    55→
    56→    # "Pack of N", "N pack", "xN", "×N"
    57→    match = re.search(r'(?:pack\s*(?:of\s*)?|x\s*|×\s*)(\d+)', title_lower)
    58→    if match:
    59→        return int(match.group(1))
    60→
    61→    # Named packs
    62→    if "twin pack" in title_lower or "duo pack" in title_lower or "double pack" in title_lower:
    63→        return 2
    64→    if "triple pack" in title_lower:
    65→        return 3
    66→    if "multipack" in title_lower:
    67→        return 2  # Conservative default
    68→
    69→    return 1
    70→
    71→
    72→def download_image(url):
    73→    """Download image and return base64-encoded bytes."""
    74→    try:
    75→        resp = http_requests.get(url, timeout=15)
    76→        if resp.status_code == 200:
    77→            content_type = resp.headers.get("content-type", "image/jpeg")
    78→            media_type = content_type.split(";")[0].strip()
    79→            if media_type not in ("image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/gif", "image/webp"):
    80→                media_type = "image/jpeg"
    81→            return base64.b64encode(resp.content).decode(), media_type
    82→        log(f"image download failed: {resp.status_code}")
    83→        return None, None
    84→    except Exception as e:
    85→        log(f"image download error: {e}")
    86→        return None, None
    87→
    88→
    89→def call_vision(image_b64, media_type, product_name, ean):
    90→    """Call Claude Vision to analyse product image."""
    91→    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    92→    try:
    93→        resp = client.messages.create(
    94→            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
    95→            max_tokens=500,
    96→            messages=[{
    97→                "role": "user",
    98→                "content": [
    99→                    {
   100→                        "type": "image",
   101→                        "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": media_type, "data": image_b64},
   102→                    },
   103→                    {
   104→                        "type": "text",
   105→                        "text": VISION_PROMPT.format(product_name=product_name, ean=ean),
   106→                    },
   107→                ],
   108→            }],
   109→        )
   110→        raw = strip_json_fences(resp.content[0].text)
   111→        return json.loads(raw)
   112→    except Exception as e:
   113→        log(f"vision call failed: {e}")
   114→        return None
   115→
   116→
   117→def verify_image(image_url, product_name, ean):
   118→    """Verify a product image. Returns dict with pass/flags/pack_size or None."""
   119→    if not image_url:
   120→        return None
   121→
   122→    image_b64, media_type = download_image(image_url)
   123→    if not image_b64:
   124→        return None
   125→
   126→    result = call_vision(image_b64, media_type, product_name, ean)
   127→    if not result:
   128→        return {"pass": False, "confidence": 0.0, "flags": ["vision_call_failed"], "pack_size_detected": 1}
   129→
   130→    return result
   131→
   132→
   133→def run(product, amazon_match, recommendation):
   134→    """Run image & listing verification on a recommendation.
   135→
   136→    Updates recommendation.image_flags and recommendation.pack_size in-place.
   137→    Only runs on Buy/Review classifications.
   138→    """
   139→    if not recommendation or recommendation.classification not in ("buy", "review"):
   140→        return recommendation
   141→
   142→    # ── Title-based pack size detection ────────────────
   143→    title_pack_size = detect_pack_size(product.name)
   144→    alt_pack_size = 1
   145→
   146→    # Check alternate ASINs for pack listings
   147→    if amazon_match and amazon_match.alternate_asins:
   148→        for alt in amazon_match.alternate_asins:
   149→            if alt.get("is_bundle") and alt.get("pack_size", 1) > 1:
   150→                alt_pack_size = alt["pack_size"]
   151→                log(f"alternate ASIN {alt['asin']} is a {alt_pack_size}-pack")
   152→                break
   153→
   154→    # ── Image verification ────────────────────────────
   155→    image_url = None
   156→    if amazon_match:
   157→        image_url = getattr(amazon_match, "image_url", None)
   158→
   159→    image_result = verify_image(image_url, product.name, product.ean)
   160→
   161→    if image_result:
   162→        recommendation.image_flags = image_result.get("flags", [])
   163→        img_pack = image_result.get("pack_size_detected", 1)
   164→
   165→        # Use highest detected pack size (most conservative)
   166→        final_pack_size = max(title_pack_size, alt_pack_size, img_pack)
   167→
   168→        if image_result.get("confidence", 1.0) < config.IMAGE_VERIFY_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD:
   169→            recommendation.image_flags.append("low_confidence_review")
   170→            log(f"low confidence ({image_result['confidence']}) — flagged for review")
   171→    else:
   172→        final_pack_size = max(title_pack_size, alt_pack_size)
   173→
   174→    recommendation.pack_size = final_pack_size
   175→    if final_pack_size > 1:
   176→        recommendation.unit_cost = round(product.buy_price / final_pack_size, 2)
   177→        log(f"pack size {final_pack_size} detected — unit cost £{recommendation.unit_cost}")
   178→
   179→    return recommendation
   180→

     9→    ./email_offers.py --vault-path "10-Email-Raw/2026-03-19-suppliers-price-list.md"
    10→    ./email_offers.py --body "EAN 4006000111827 Nivea Soft £3.50 MOQ 10"
    11→"""

    14→import os

    19→

    21→
    22→from config import QUEUE_PENDING
    23→from vault import log, vault_read
    24→
    25→ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "")
    26→
    27→EXTRACT_PROMPT = """You are a product data extraction agent for ArryBarry Health & Beauty.
    28→
    29→Extract ALL products from this supplier email/price list. For each product, extract:
    30→- ean: EAN/GTIN barcode (13 digits). If not present, leave empty string.
    31→- name: Product name/description
    32→- brand: Brand name
    33→- buy_price: Price per unit (numeric only, no currency symbols)
    34→- currency: GBP or EUR (detect from context/symbols)
    35→- moq: Minimum order quantity (default 1 if not stated)
    36→
    37→Respond with JSON array only, no markdown:
    38→[{"ean": "4006000111827", "name": "Nivea Soft 500ml", "brand": "Nivea", "buy_price": 3.50, "currency": "GBP", "moq": 10}]
    39→
    40→If no products can be extracted, return: []
    41→
    42→Email content:
    43→"""
    44→
    45→def is_qogita_price_alert(body):
    46→    """Detect Qogita price alert notification emails from body content."""
    47→    if not body:
    48→        return False
    49→    body_lower = body.lower()[:1000]
    50→    if 'qogita' not in body_lower:
    51→        return False
    52→    return any(kw in body_lower for kw in [
    53→        'price alert', 'price drop', 'price notification',
    54→        'target price reached', 'price has dropped', 'price has been reduced',
    55→    ])
    56→
    57→
    58→def extract_price_alert_data(body):
    59→    """Extract product + price from a Qogita price alert email body."""
    60→    import anthropic
    61→    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    62→    prompt = """Extract the product and new price from this Qogita price alert email.
    63→Return a JSON object: {"product_name": "...", "ean": "...", "new_price": number, "currency": "EUR or GBP"}
    64→If you can't extract, return null."""
    65→
    66→    try:
    67→        resp = client.messages.create(
    68→            model='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
    69→            max_tokens=300,
    70→            messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': prompt + '\n\nEmail:\n' + body[:2000]}],
    71→        )
    72→        raw = resp.content[0].text.strip()
    73→        raw = re.sub(r'^```[a-z]*\n?', '', raw, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    74→        raw = re.sub(r'\n?```$', '', raw)
    75→        return json.loads(raw.strip())
    76→    except Exception as e:
    77→        log(f'price alert extraction failed: {e}')
    78→        return None
    79→
    80→
    81→def handle_price_alert(alert_data):
    82→    """Process a triggered price alert — re-analyse and optionally auto-execute."""
    83→    from alerts.qogita_alerts import load_active_alerts, save_active_alerts
    84→    from execute import has_prior_approval
    85→
    86→    ean = alert_data.get('ean')
    87→    new_price = alert_data.get('new_price')
    88→    if not ean or not new_price:
    89→        log('incomplete alert data, skipping')
    90→        return
    91→
    92→    active = load_active_alerts()
    93→    tracked = next((a for a in active if a['ean'] == ean), None)
    94→
    95→    if not tracked:
    96→        log(f'untracked alert for {ean}, adding to queue anyway')
    97→
    98→    # Convert EUR to GBP if needed
    99→    if alert_data.get('currency') == 'EUR':
   100→        from fx import convert
   101→        new_price = convert(new_price, 'EUR', 'GBP')
   102→        if not new_price:
   103→            log(f'FX conversion failed for alert {ean}')
   104→            return
   105→
   106→    # Queue for re-analysis with new price
   107→    auto_exec = has_prior_approval(ean)
   108→    from queue_manager import enqueue
   109→    enqueue({
   110→        'product': {'ean': ean, 'name': alert_data.get('product_name', ''),
   111→                     'buy_price': new_price, 'currency': 'GBP'},
   112→        'source': 'price-alert',
   113→        'supplier': 'Qogita',
   114→        'priority': True,
   115→        'auto_execute': auto_exec,
   116→    })
   117→    log(f'price alert queued: {ean} at £{new_price:.2f} (auto_execute={auto_exec})')
   118→
   119→    # Remove from active alerts
   120→    if tracked:
   121→        active = [a for a in active if a['ean'] != ean]
   122→        save_active_alerts(active)
   123→
   124→
   125→
   126→
   127→def extract_products(text: str) -> list[dict]:
   128→    """Use Claude to extract products from email text."""
   129→    if not ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:
   130→        log("email_offers: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set")
   131→        return []
   132→
   133→    import anthropic
   134→    client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
   135→
   136→    try:
   137→        response = client.messages.create(
   138→            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   139→            max_tokens=4000,
   140→            temperature=0,
   141→            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": EXTRACT_PROMPT + text[:4000]}],
   142→        )
   143→        raw = response.content[0].text.strip()
   144→        # Strip markdown fences if present (handles ```json etc.)
   145→        raw = re.sub(r"^```[a-z]*\n?", "", raw, flags=re.MULTILINE)
   146→        raw = re.sub(r"\n?```$", "", raw)
   147→        products = json.loads(raw)
   148→        if isinstance(products, list):
   149→            log(f"email_offers: extracted {len(products)} products via Claude")
   150→            return products
   151→    except Exception as e:
   152→        log(f"email_offers: extraction failed: {e}")
   153→    return []
   154→
   155→
   156→def process_email(vault_path: str = "", body: str = "", supplier: str = "Email Supplier",
   157→                  dry_run: bool = False) -> int:
   158→    """Process a supplier offer email. Returns count of products queued."""
   159→    if vault_path:
   160→        content = vault_read(vault_path)
   161→        if not content:
   162→            log(f"email_offers: cannot read vault path: {vault_path}")
   163→            return 0
   164→        # Extract supplier from frontmatter or filename
   165→        if "from:" in content.lower():
   166→            for line in content.split("\n"):
   167→                if line.lower().startswith("from:"):
   168→                    supplier = line.split(":", 1)[1].strip()
   169→                    break
   170→        body = content
   171→    elif not body:
   172→        log("email_offers: no input provided")

   174→

   176→    if is_qogita_price_alert(body):

   178→        if alert_data:
   179→            handle_price_alert(alert_data)
   180→            return 0  # Handled as price alert, not a supplier offer
   181→
   182→    products = extract_products(body)
   183→    if not products:
   184→        return 0
   185→
   186→    # Normalise
   187→    normalised = []
   188→    for p in products:
   189→        if not p.get("ean") and not p.get("name"):
   190→            continue
   191→        normalised.append({
   192→            "ean": str(p.get("ean", "")).strip(),
   193→            "name": p.get("name", ""),
   194→            "brand": p.get("brand", ""),
   195→            "buy_price": float(p.get("buy_price", 0)),
   196→            "currency": p.get("currency", "GBP"),

   203→    if not normalised:

   205→
   206→    if dry_run:
   207→        for p in normalised:
   208→            log(f"  dry-run: {p['ean']} {p['name'][:40]} @ £{p['buy_price']:.2f}")
   209→        return len(normalised)
   210→
   211→    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   212→    opp_id = f"email-{now.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}"
   213→    opp = {
   214→        "id": opp_id,
   215→        "source": "email",
   216→        "supplier": supplier,
   217→        "received_at": now.isoformat(),
   218→        "products": normalised,

   220→    os.makedirs(QUEUE_PENDING, exist_ok=True)
   221→    with open(os.path.join(QUEUE_PENDING, f"{opp_id}.json"), "w") as f:
   222→        json.dump(opp, f, indent=2)
   223→    log(f"email_offers: queued {len(normalised)} products from {supplier}")
   224→    return len(normalised)

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""vault-intel adapter — extract competitor pricing from classified vault notes.
     3→
     4→Reads notes from:
     5→  - 07-Marketplace/Buying/Competitor/
     6→  - 04-Operations/Email-Intelligence/
     7→Extracts structured pricing signals via Claude Haiku.
     8→Outputs enrichment JSON to data/product-intel/competitor/ keyed by EAN.
     9→
    10→Cron: daily at 7am.
    11→"""
    12→import json
    13→import subprocess
    14→from datetime import datetime
    15→
    16→import anthropic
    17→
    18→import sys
    19→import os
    20→sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))))
    21→import config
    22→from vault import log, strip_json_fences
    23→
    24→SKILL = "opportunity-analyser"
    25→VAULT_FOLDERS = [
    26→    "07-Marketplace/Buying/Competitor/",
    27→    "04-Operations/Email-Intelligence/",
    28→]
    29→
    30→EXTRACT_PROMPT = """Extract ALL competitor/supplier pricing signals from this vault note.
    31→Return a JSON array of objects with these fields:
    32→- ean: product EAN/GTIN (string, or null if not found)
    33→- product: product name
    34→- competitor: company/seller name
    35→- price: price as a number (GBP)
    36→- marketplace: where seen (amazon, ebay, wholesale, qogita, etc.)
    37→- date: date the pricing was observed (YYYY-MM-DD)
    38→
    39→If no pricing signals found, return an empty array [].
    40→Only return the JSON array, no other text."""
    41→
    42→
    43→
    44→
    45→def call_haiku(text, note_path):
    46→    """Extract pricing signals from note text using Claude Haiku."""
    47→    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    48→    try:
    49→        resp = client.messages.create(
    50→            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
    51→            max_tokens=2000,
    52→            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"Note: {note_path}\n\n{text}\n\n{EXTRACT_PROMPT}"}],
    53→        )
    54→        raw = resp.content[0].text.strip()
    55→        raw = strip_json_fences(raw)
    56→        return json.loads(raw)
    57→    except Exception as e:
    58→        log(f"haiku extraction failed for {note_path}: {e}")
    59→        return []
    60→
    61→
    62→def extract_pricing_signals(text, note_path):
    63→    """Extract pricing signals from a vault note."""
    64→    return call_haiku(text, note_path)
    65→
    66→
    67→# ── Dedup ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→
    69→def _processed_file(data_dir):
    70→    return os.path.join(data_dir, "competitor", "processed-notes.json")
    71→
    72→
    73→def _load_processed(data_dir):
    74→    path = _processed_file(data_dir)
    75→    if os.path.exists(path):
    76→        with open(path) as f:
    77→            return json.load(f)
    78→    return {}
    79→
    80→
    81→def _save_processed(index, data_dir):
    82→    path = _processed_file(data_dir)
    83→    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)
    84→    with open(path, "w") as f:
    85→        json.dump(index, f, indent=2)
    86→
    87→
    88→def is_processed(note_path, last_modified, data_dir=None):
    89→    data_dir = data_dir or config.INTEL_ROOT
    90→    index = _load_processed(data_dir)
    91→    return index.get(note_path) == last_modified
    92→
    93→
    94→def mark_processed(note_path, last_modified, data_dir=None):
    95→    data_dir = data_dir or config.INTEL_ROOT
    96→    index = _load_processed(data_dir)
    97→    index[note_path] = last_modified
    98→    _save_processed(index, data_dir)
    99→
   100→
   101→# ── Vault I/O ─────────────────────────────────────────
   102→
   103→def vault_list_notes(folder):
   104→    """List notes in a vault folder via obsidian-sync."""
   105→    try:
   106→        result = subprocess.run(
   107→            ["bash", "/opt/bigman-engine/skills/active/obsidian-sync/sync.sh", "list", folder],
   108→            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30,
   109→        )
   110→        if result.returncode != 0:
   111→            log(f"vault list failed for {folder}: {result.stderr[:200]}")
   112→            return []
   113→        lines = [l.strip() for l in result.stdout.strip().split("\n") if l.strip()]
   114→        return lines
   115→    except Exception as e:
   116→        log(f"vault list error: {e}")
   117→        return []
   118→
   119→
   120→def vault_read_note(path):
   121→    """Read a vault note via obsidian-sync."""
   122→    try:
   123→        result = subprocess.run(
   124→            ["bash", "/opt/bigman-engine/skills/active/obsidian-sync/sync.sh", "get", path],
   125→            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30,
   126→        )
   127→        if result.returncode != 0:
   128→            log(f"vault read failed for {path}: {result.stderr[:200]}")
   129→            return None
   130→        return result.stdout
   131→    except Exception as e:
   132→        log(f"vault read error: {e}")
   133→        return None
   134→
   135→
   136→# ── Enrichment Output ─────────────────────────────────
   137→
   138→def save_competitor_data(signals, data_dir=None):
   139→    """Save extracted signals to product-intel/competitor/ keyed by EAN."""
   140→    data_dir = data_dir or config.INTEL_ROOT
   141→    comp_dir = os.path.join(data_dir, "competitor")
   142→    os.makedirs(comp_dir, exist_ok=True)
   143→
   144→    # Group signals by EAN to avoid N+1 file reads/writes
   145→    by_ean = {}
   146→    for signal in signals:
   147→        ean = signal.get("ean")
   148→        if ean:
   149→            by_ean.setdefault(ean, []).append(signal)
   150→
   151→    for ean, ean_signals in by_ean.items():
   152→        path = os.path.join(comp_dir, f"{ean}.json")
   153→        existing = []
   154→        if os.path.exists(path):
   155→            with open(path) as f:
   156→                existing = json.load(f)
   157→        existing_keys = {f"{s.get('competitor')}:{s.get('date')}:{s.get('price')}" for s in existing}
   158→        changed = False
   159→        for signal in ean_signals:
   160→            key = f"{signal.get('competitor')}:{signal.get('date')}:{signal.get('price')}"
   161→            if key not in existing_keys:
   162→                existing.append(signal)
   163→                existing_keys.add(key)
   164→                changed = True
   165→        if changed:
   166→            with open(path, "w") as f:
   167→                json.dump(existing, f, indent=2)
   168→
   169→
   170→# ── Main ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   171→
   172→def run(dry_run=False, data_dir=None):
   173→    """Main entry point — scan vault folders, extract pricing, save."""
   174→    data_dir = data_dir or config.INTEL_ROOT
   175→    total_signals = 0
   176→    total_notes = 0
   177→
   178→    for folder in VAULT_FOLDERS:
   179→        notes = vault_list_notes(folder)
   180→        log(f"{folder}: {len(notes)} notes found")
   181→
   182→        for note_path in notes:
   183→            modified = datetime.now().isoformat()
   184→            if is_processed(note_path, modified, data_dir):
   185→                continue
   186→
   187→            text = vault_read_note(note_path)
   188→            if not text or len(text.strip()) < 50:
   189→                mark_processed(note_path, modified, data_dir)
   190→                continue
   191→
   192→            total_notes += 1
   193→            if dry_run:
   194→                log(f"[dry-run] would extract from: {note_path}")
   195→                continue
   196→
   197→            signals = extract_pricing_signals(text, note_path)
   198→            if signals:
   199→                save_competitor_data(signals, data_dir)
   200→                total_signals += len(signals)
   201→                log(f"{note_path}: extracted {len(signals)} pricing signals")
   202→
   203→            mark_processed(note_path, modified, data_dir)
   204→
   205→    log(f"done: {total_notes} notes scanned, {total_signals} pricing signals extracted")
   206→
   207→
   208→if __name__ == "__main__":
   209→    dry = "--dry-run" in sys.argv
   210→    run(dry_run=dry)
   211→

---
name: opportunity-analyser
description: Analyse wholesale product opportunities against Amazon marketplace data. Checks compliance, calculates margins, estimates volumes, scores Buy/Review/Skip. Use when evaluating products to purchase for resale.
---

# Opportunity Analyser

Source-agnostic product opportunity analysis pipeline. Takes products from any source (Qogita, supplier emails, pricing files) and analyses them against Amazon for compliance, margins, demand, and purchase recommendations.

## Usage

### Manual single-product analysis
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/analyse.py \
  --ean 4006000111827 --price 3.50 --supplier "Qogita" --brand "Nivea" --name "Nivea Soft 500ml"
```

### Process pending queue
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/analyse.py --queue
```

### Dry run (no vault writes, no file moves)
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/analyse.py --queue --dry-run
```

## Cron
```cron
0 */2 * * * cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 skills/active/opportunity-analyser/analyse.py --queue >> /var/log/opportunity-analyser.log 2>&1
```

## Output
- Recommendations: `07-Marketplace/Buying/Recommendations/`
- Daily summary: `07-Marketplace/Buying/Reports/`
- Raw predictors: `/opt/bigman-engine/data/product-intel/predictors/`

## Secrets (Doppler shared-services)
- `SP_API_REFRESH_TOKEN`, `SP_API_CLIENT_ID`, `SP_API_CLIENT_SECRET`, `SP_API_SELLER_ID`, `SP_API_AWS_ACCESS_KEY`, `SP_API_AWS_SECRET_KEY`
- `QOGITA_BUYER_EMAIL`, `QOGITA_BUYER_PASSWORD`
- `RAINFOREST_API_KEY`
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (existing)

## Source Adapters

### Qogita Scan — daily wholesale catalog scan
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/adapters/qogita_scan.py --dry-run
```
Cron: `0 6 * * *` — scans all Qogita offers, converts EUR→GBP, dedup, writes to pending queue.

### File Ingest — CSV/Excel/PDF price lists
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/adapters/file_ingest.py --dry-run
```
Cron: `*/30 * * * *` — watches `data/opportunities/inbox/` for .csv/.xlsx/.pdf files.
Single file: `--file /path/to/pricelist.csv`

### Email Offers — supplier email extraction
Triggered automatically by email-triage when a `suppliers` email contains pricing keywords.
Manual: `--vault-path "10-Email-Raw/2026-03-19-suppliers-price-list.md"`

## Execution Layer

### Approve opportunity for purchase
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/execute.py --approve <opportunity-id>
```

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Extract job ID from branch name (job/uuid -> uuid), fallback to random UUID
if [[ "$BRANCH" == job/* ]]; then
    JOB_ID="${BRANCH#job/}"
else
    JOB_ID=$(cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid)
fi
echo "Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"

# Export SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars (GH_TOKEN, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, etc.)
if [ -n "$SECRETS" ]; then
    eval $(echo "$SECRETS" | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)"')
fi

# Export LLM_SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars
if [ -n "$LLM_SECRETS" ]; then
    eval $(echo "$LLM_SECRETS" | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)"')
fi

# Unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY so Claude Code uses the OAuth token.
# If both are set, Claude Code prioritizes API key (billing to API credits)
# which defeats the purpose. The API key is for the event handler, not here.
unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

# Git setup - derive identity from GitHub token
gh auth setup-git
GH_USER_JSON=$(gh api user -q '{name: .name, login: .login, email: .email, id: .id}')
GH_USER_NAME=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.name // .login')
GH_USER_EMAIL=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.email // "\(.id)+\(.login)@users.noreply.github.com"')
git config --global user.name "$GH_USER_NAME"
git config --global user.email "$GH_USER_EMAIL"

# Clone branch
if [ -n "$REPO_URL" ]; then
    git clone --single-branch --branch "$BRANCH" --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" /job
else
    echo "No REPO_URL provided"
fi

cd /job

WORKSPACE_DIR=$(pwd)

# Skip onboarding and trust dialogs
mkdir -p ~/.claude
cat > ~/.claude/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "theme": "dark",
  "hasTrustDialogAccepted": true,
  "skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt": true
}
EOF

cat > ~/.claude.json << ENDJSON
{
  "hasCompletedOnboarding": true,
  "projects": {
    "${WORKSPACE_DIR}": {
      "allowedTools": ["WebSearch"],
      "hasTrustDialogAccepted": true,
      "hasTrustDialogHooksAccepted": true
    }
  }
}
ENDJSON

# Install npm deps for active skills (native deps need correct Linux arch)
for skill_dir in /job/skills/active/*/; do
    if [ -f "${skill_dir}package.json" ]; then
        echo "Installing skill deps: $(basename "$skill_dir")"
        (cd "$skill_dir" && npm install --omit=dev --no-package-lock)
    fi
done

# Register Playwright MCP server (browser automation via native Claude Code tools)
claude mcp add --transport stdio playwright -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest --headless --browser chromium

# Setup logs
LOG_DIR="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}"
mkdir -p "${LOG_DIR}"

# Build system prompt from config MD files
SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/system-prompt.md"
SYSTEM_FILES=("SOUL.md" "JOB_AGENT.md")
> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
for i in "${!SYSTEM_FILES[@]}"; do
    cat "/job/config/${SYSTEM_FILES[$i]}" >> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
    if [ "$i" -lt $((${#SYSTEM_FILES[@]} - 1)) ]; then
        echo -e "\n\n" >> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
    fi
done

# Resolve {{datetime}} variable in system prompt
sed -i "s/{{datetime}}/$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")/g" "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"

# Read job metadata from job.config.json
JOB_CONFIG="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}/job.config.json"
TITLE=$(jq -r '.title // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")
JOB_DESCRIPTION=$(jq -r '.job // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")

PROMPT="

# Your Job

${JOB_DESCRIPTION}"

# Build --model flag if LLM_MODEL is set
MODEL_FLAG=""
if [ -n "$LLM_MODEL" ]; then
    MODEL_FLAG="--model $LLM_MODEL"
fi

# Run Claude Code — capture exit code instead of letting set -e kill the script
# stream-json gives the full conversation trace (thinking, tool calls, results)
# similar to Pi's .jsonl session logs
set +e
claude -p "$PROMPT" \
    $MODEL_FLAG \
    --append-system-prompt-file "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE" \
    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
    --verbose \
    --output-format stream-json \
    > "${LOG_DIR}/claude-session.jsonl" 2>"${LOG_DIR}/claude-stderr.log"
AGENT_EXIT=$?

# Commit based on outcome
if [ $AGENT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    # Claude Code failed — only commit session logs, not partial code changes
    git reset || true
    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE} (failed)" || true
else
    # Claude Code succeeded — commit everything
    git add -A
    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" || true
fi

git push origin

# Capture log commit SHA, then remove logs so they don't merge into main
LOG_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
git rm -rf "${LOG_DIR}"
git commit -m "done." || true
git push origin
set -e

# Create PR with log permalink (auto-merge handled by GitHub Actions workflow)
REPO_SLUG=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner)
LOG_URL="https://github.com/${REPO_SLUG}/tree/${LOG_SHA}/logs/${JOB_ID}"
gh pr create --title "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" \
  --body "📋 [View Job Logs](${LOG_URL})"$'\n\n---\n\n'"${JOB_DESCRIPTION}" \
  --base main || true

# Re-raise failure so the workflow reports it
if [ $AGENT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Claude Code exited with code ${AGENT_EXIT}"
    exit $AGENT_EXIT
fi

echo "Done. Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Extract job ID from branch name (job/uuid -> uuid), fallback to random UUID
if [[ "$BRANCH" == job/* ]]; then
    JOB_ID="${BRANCH#job/}"
else
    JOB_ID=$(cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid)
fi
echo "Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"

# Export SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars (GH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.)
# These are filtered from LLM's bash subprocess by env-sanitizer extension
if [ -n "$SECRETS" ]; then
    eval $(echo "$SECRETS" | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)"')
fi

# Export LLM_SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars
# These are NOT filtered - LLM can access these (browser logins, skill API keys, etc.)
if [ -n "$LLM_SECRETS" ]; then
    eval $(echo "$LLM_SECRETS" | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)"')
fi

# Git setup - derive identity from GitHub token
gh auth setup-git
GH_USER_JSON=$(gh api user -q '{name: .name, login: .login, email: .email, id: .id}')
GH_USER_NAME=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.name // .login')
GH_USER_EMAIL=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.email // "\(.id)+\(.login)@users.noreply.github.com"')
git config --global user.name "$GH_USER_NAME"
git config --global user.email "$GH_USER_EMAIL"

# Clone branch
if [ -n "$REPO_URL" ]; then
    git clone --single-branch --branch "$BRANCH" --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" /job
else
    echo "No REPO_URL provided"
fi

cd /job

# Install npm deps for active skills (native deps need correct Linux arch)
for skill_dir in /job/skills/active/*/; do
    if [ -f "${skill_dir}package.json" ]; then
        echo "Installing skill deps: $(basename "$skill_dir")"
        (cd "$skill_dir" && npm install --omit=dev --no-package-lock)
    fi
done

# Start Chrome if available (installed by browser-tools skill via Puppeteer)
CHROME_PID=""
CHROME_BIN=$(find /home/agent/.cache/puppeteer -name "chrome" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
if [ -n "$CHROME_BIN" ]; then
    $CHROME_BIN --headless --no-sandbox --disable-gpu --remote-debugging-port=9222 2>/dev/null &
    CHROME_PID=$!
    sleep 2
fi

# Setup logs
LOG_DIR="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}"
mkdir -p "${LOG_DIR}"

# 1. Build system prompt from config MD files
SYSTEM_FILES=("SOUL.md" "JOB_AGENT.md")
> /job/.pi/SYSTEM.md
for i in "${!SYSTEM_FILES[@]}"; do
    cat "/job/config/${SYSTEM_FILES[$i]}" >> /job/.pi/SYSTEM.md
    if [ "$i" -lt $((${#SYSTEM_FILES[@]} - 1)) ]; then
        echo -e "\n\n" >> /job/.pi/SYSTEM.md
    fi
done

# Resolve {{datetime}} variable in SYSTEM.md
sed -i "s/{{datetime}}/$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")/g" /job/.pi/SYSTEM.md

# Read job metadata from job.config.json
JOB_CONFIG="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}/job.config.json"
TITLE=$(jq -r '.title // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")
JOB_DESCRIPTION=$(jq -r '.job // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")

PROMPT="

# Your Job

${JOB_DESCRIPTION}"

LLM_PROVIDER="${LLM_PROVIDER:-anthropic}"

MODEL_FLAGS="--provider $LLM_PROVIDER"
if [ -n "$LLM_MODEL" ]; then
    MODEL_FLAGS="$MODEL_FLAGS --model $LLM_MODEL"
fi

# Generate models.json for custom provider (OpenAI-compatible endpoints like Ollama)
if [ "$LLM_PROVIDER" = "custom" ] && [ -n "$OPENAI_BASE_URL" ]; then
    # If no API key was provided, set a dummy so Pi doesn't send empty auth
    if [ -z "$CUSTOM_API_KEY" ]; then
        export CUSTOM_API_KEY="not-needed"
    fi
    cat > /home/agent/.pi/agent/models.json <<MODELS
{
  "providers": {
    "custom": {
      "baseUrl": "$OPENAI_BASE_URL",
      "api": "openai-completions",
      "apiKey": "CUSTOM_API_KEY",
      "models": [{ "id": "$LLM_MODEL" }]
    }
  }
}
MODELS
fi

# Copy custom models.json to PI's global config if present in repo (overrides generated)
if [ -f "/job/.pi/agent/models.json" ]; then
    mkdir -p /home/agent/.pi/agent
    cp /job/.pi/agent/models.json /home/agent/.pi/agent/models.json
fi

# Run Pi — capture exit code instead of letting set -e kill the script
set +e
pi $MODEL_FLAGS -p "$PROMPT" --session-dir "${LOG_DIR}"
PI_EXIT=$?

# 2. Commit based on outcome
if [ $PI_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    # Pi failed — only commit session logs, not partial code changes
    git reset || true
    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE} (failed)" || true
else
    # Pi succeeded — commit everything
    git add -A
    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" || true
fi

git push origin

# Capture log commit SHA, then remove logs so they don't merge into main
LOG_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
git rm -rf "${LOG_DIR}"
git commit -m "done." || true
git push origin
set -e

# Create PR with log permalink (auto-merge handled by GitHub Actions workflow)
REPO_SLUG=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner)
LOG_URL="https://github.com/${REPO_SLUG}/tree/${LOG_SHA}/logs/${JOB_ID}"
gh pr create --title "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" \
  --body "📋 [View Job Logs](${LOG_URL})"$'\n\n---\n\n'"${JOB_DESCRIPTION}" \
  --base main || true

# Cleanup
if [ -n "$CHROME_PID" ]; then
    kill $CHROME_PID 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Re-raise Pi's failure so the workflow reports it
if [ $PI_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Pi exited with code ${PI_EXIT}"
    exit $PI_EXIT
fi

echo "Done. Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"

FROM node:22-bookworm-slim

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    git \
    jq \
    curl \
    procps \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

# Claude Code refuses --dangerously-skip-permissions as root.
# Create a non-root user and give it ownership of /job.
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent \
    && mkdir -p /job \
    && chown agent:agent /job

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh

# Install Playwright Chromium + system deps at build time (used by Playwright MCP)
# PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH puts browsers in a shared location so the non-root
# `agent` user can find what root installed during build.
ENV PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/opt/pw-browsers
RUN npx -y playwright install --with-deps chromium \
    && chown -R agent:agent /opt/pw-browsers

USER agent
WORKDIR /job

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

FROM node:22-bookworm-slim

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    git \
    jq \
    curl \
    procps \
    # Chrome/Chromium dependencies
    libnss3 \
    libnspr4 \
    libatk1.0-0 \
    libatk-bridge2.0-0 \
    libcups2 \
    libdrm2 \
    libdbus-1-3 \
    libxkbcommon0 \
    libatspi2.0-0 \
    libxcomposite1 \
    libxdamage1 \
    libxfixes3 \
    libxrandr2 \
    libgbm1 \
    libasound2 \
    libpango-1.0-0 \
    libcairo2 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN npm install -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent

# Create a non-root user and give it ownership of /job.
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent \
    && mkdir -p /job \
    && chown agent:agent /job

# Create Pi config directory (extension loaded from repo at runtime)
RUN mkdir -p /home/agent/.pi/agent \
    && chown -R agent:agent /home/agent/.pi

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh

USER agent
WORKDIR /job

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

---
name: opportunity-analyser
description: Analyse wholesale product opportunities against Amazon marketplace data. Checks compliance, calculates margins, estimates volumes, scores Buy/Review/Skip. Use when evaluating products to purchase for resale.
---

# Opportunity Analyser

Source-agnostic product opportunity analysis pipeline. Takes products from any source (Qogita, supplier emails, pricing files) and analyses them against Amazon for compliance, margins, demand, and purchase recommendations.

## Usage

### Manual single-product analysis
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/analyse.py \
  --ean 4006000111827 --price 3.50 --supplier "Qogita" --brand "Nivea" --name "Nivea Soft 500ml"
```

### Process pending queue
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/analyse.py --queue
```

### Dry run (no vault writes, no file moves)
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/analyse.py --queue --dry-run
```

## Cron
```cron
0 */2 * * * cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 skills/active/opportunity-analyser/analyse.py --queue >> /var/log/opportunity-analyser.log 2>&1
```

## Output
- Recommendations: `07-Marketplace/Buying/Recommendations/`
- Daily summary: `07-Marketplace/Buying/Reports/`
- Raw predictors: `/opt/bigman-engine/data/product-intel/predictors/`

## Secrets (Doppler shared-services)
- `SP_API_REFRESH_TOKEN`, `SP_API_CLIENT_ID`, `SP_API_CLIENT_SECRET`, `SP_API_SELLER_ID`, `SP_API_AWS_ACCESS_KEY`, `SP_API_AWS_SECRET_KEY`
- `QOGITA_BUYER_EMAIL`, `QOGITA_BUYER_PASSWORD`
- `RAINFOREST_API_KEY`
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (existing)

## Source Adapters

### Qogita Scan — daily wholesale catalog scan
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/adapters/qogita_scan.py --dry-run
```
Cron: `0 6 * * *` — scans all Qogita offers, converts EUR→GBP, dedup, writes to pending queue.

### File Ingest — CSV/Excel/PDF price lists
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/adapters/file_ingest.py --dry-run
```
Cron: `*/30 * * * *` — watches `data/opportunities/inbox/` for .csv/.xlsx/.pdf files.
Single file: `--file /path/to/pricelist.csv`

### Email Offers — supplier email extraction
Triggered automatically by email-triage when a `suppliers` email contains pricing keywords.
Manual: `--vault-path "10-Email-Raw/2026-03-19-suppliers-price-list.md"`

## Execution Layer

### Approve opportunity for purchase
```bash
cd /opt/bigman-engine && doppler run -p shared-services -c prd -- \
  venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3 \
  skills/active/opportunity-analyser/execute.py --approve <opportunity-id>
```
- Qogita source: adds to cart. Run with `--confirm` to create checkout.
- Other sources: generates draft PO in vault (`07-Marketplace/Buying/Orders/`).
- `--qty 50` to override quantity. `--list` to see pending approvals.

## 6-Stage Pipeline
1. **Amazon Match** — EAN→ASIN, fuzzy fallback, brand mismatch filtering
2. **Compliance** — SP-API restrictions, hazmat, IP, regulatory flags
3. **Market Analysis** — BSR, sellers, sales estimates, pricing (SP-API + Rainforest backup)
4. **Margin Calc** — Amazon fees, ROI, profit per unit
5. **Volume Estimate** — purchase quantity (sales velocity → BSR estimate → MOQ fallback)
6. **Scoring** — configurable thresholds → Buy/Review/Skip

FROM node:22-bookworm-slim

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    git \
    jq \
    curl \
    procps \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg | dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list > /dev/null \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

# Claude Code refuses --dangerously-skip-permissions as root.
# Create a non-root user and give it ownership of /job.
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent \
    && mkdir -p /job \
    && chown agent:agent /job

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh

# Install Playwright Chromium + system deps at build time (used by Playwright MCP)
# PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH puts browsers in a shared location so the non-root
# `agent` user can find what root installed during build.
ENV PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/opt/pw-browsers
RUN npx -y playwright install --with-deps chromium \
    && chown -R agent:agent /opt/pw-browsers

USER agent
WORKDIR /job

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Extract job ID from branch name (job/uuid -> uuid), fallback to random UUID
if [[ "$BRANCH" == job/* ]]; then
    JOB_ID="${BRANCH#job/}"
else
    JOB_ID=$(cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid)
fi
echo "Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"

# Export SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars (GH_TOKEN, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, etc.)
if [ -n "$SECRETS" ]; then
    eval $(echo "$SECRETS" | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)"')
fi

# Export LLM_SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars
if [ -n "$LLM_SECRETS" ]; then
    eval $(echo "$LLM_SECRETS" | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)"')
fi

# Unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY so Claude Code uses the OAuth token.
# If both are set, Claude Code prioritizes API key (billing to API credits)
# which defeats the purpose. The API key is for the event handler, not here.
unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

# Git setup - derive identity from GitHub token
gh auth setup-git
GH_USER_JSON=$(gh api user -q '{name: .name, login: .login, email: .email, id: .id}')
GH_USER_NAME=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.name // .login')
GH_USER_EMAIL=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.email // "\(.id)+\(.login)@users.noreply.github.com"')
git config --global user.name "$GH_USER_NAME"
git config --global user.email "$GH_USER_EMAIL"

# Clone branch
if [ -n "$REPO_URL" ]; then
    git clone --single-branch --branch "$BRANCH" --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" /job
else
    echo "No REPO_URL provided"
fi

cd /job

WORKSPACE_DIR=$(pwd)

# Skip onboarding and trust dialogs
mkdir -p ~/.claude
cat > ~/.claude/settings.json << 'EOF'
{
  "theme": "dark",
  "hasTrustDialogAccepted": true,
  "skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt": true
}
EOF

cat > ~/.claude.json << ENDJSON
{
  "hasCompletedOnboarding": true,
  "projects": {
    "${WORKSPACE_DIR}": {
      "allowedTools": ["WebSearch"],
      "hasTrustDialogAccepted": true,
      "hasTrustDialogHooksAccepted": true
    }
  }
}
ENDJSON

# Install npm deps for active skills (native deps need correct Linux arch)
for skill_dir in /job/skills/active/*/; do
    if [ -f "${skill_dir}package.json" ]; then
        echo "Installing skill deps: $(basename "$skill_dir")"
        (cd "$skill_dir" && npm install --omit=dev --no-package-lock)
    fi
done

# Register Playwright MCP server (browser automation via native Claude Code tools)
claude mcp add --transport stdio playwright -- npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest --headless --browser chromium

# Setup logs
LOG_DIR="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}"
mkdir -p "${LOG_DIR}"

# Build system prompt from config MD files
SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/system-prompt.md"
SYSTEM_FILES=("SOUL.md" "JOB_AGENT.md")
> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
for i in "${!SYSTEM_FILES[@]}"; do
    cat "/job/config/${SYSTEM_FILES[$i]}" >> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
    if [ "$i" -lt $((${#SYSTEM_FILES[@]} - 1)) ]; then
        echo -e "\n\n" >> "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"
    fi
done

# Resolve {{datetime}} variable in system prompt
sed -i "s/{{datetime}}/$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")/g" "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE"

# Read job metadata from job.config.json
JOB_CONFIG="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}/job.config.json"
TITLE=$(jq -r '.title // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")
JOB_DESCRIPTION=$(jq -r '.job // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")

PROMPT="

# Your Job

${JOB_DESCRIPTION}"

# Build --model flag if LLM_MODEL is set
MODEL_FLAG=""
if [ -n "$LLM_MODEL" ]; then
    MODEL_FLAG="--model $LLM_MODEL"
fi

# Run Claude Code — capture exit code instead of letting set -e kill the script
# stream-json gives the full conversation trace (thinking, tool calls, results)
# similar to Pi's .jsonl session logs
set +e
claude -p "$PROMPT" \
    $MODEL_FLAG \
    --append-system-prompt-file "$SYSTEM_PROMPT_FILE" \
    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
    --verbose \
    --output-format stream-json \
    > "${LOG_DIR}/claude-session.jsonl" 2>"${LOG_DIR}/claude-stderr.log"
AGENT_EXIT=$?

# Commit based on outcome
if [ $AGENT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    # Claude Code failed — only commit session logs, not partial code changes
    git reset || true
    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE} (failed)" || true
else
    # Claude Code succeeded — commit everything
    git add -A
    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" || true
fi

git push origin

# Capture log commit SHA, then remove logs so they don't merge into main
LOG_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
git rm -rf "${LOG_DIR}"
git commit -m "done." || true
git push origin
set -e

# Create PR with log permalink (auto-merge handled by GitHub Actions workflow)
REPO_SLUG=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner)
LOG_URL="https://github.com/${REPO_SLUG}/tree/${LOG_SHA}/logs/${JOB_ID}"
gh pr create --title "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" \
  --body "📋 [View Job Logs](${LOG_URL})"$'\n\n---\n\n'"${JOB_DESCRIPTION}" \
  --base main || true

# Re-raise failure so the workflow reports it
if [ $AGENT_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Claude Code exited with code ${AGENT_EXIT}"
    exit $AGENT_EXIT
fi

echo "Done. Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"

# Extract job ID from branch name (job/uuid -> uuid), fallback to random UUID
if [[ "$BRANCH" == job/* ]]; then
    JOB_ID="${BRANCH#job/}"
else
    JOB_ID=$(cat /proc/sys/kernel/random/uuid)
fi
echo "Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"

# Export SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars (GH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, etc.)
# These are filtered from LLM's bash subprocess by env-sanitizer extension
if [ -n "$SECRETS" ]; then
    eval $(echo "$SECRETS" | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)"')
fi

# Export LLM_SECRETS (JSON) as flat env vars
# These are NOT filtered - LLM can access these (browser logins, skill API keys, etc.)
if [ -n "$LLM_SECRETS" ]; then
    eval $(echo "$LLM_SECRETS" | jq -r 'to_entries | .[] | "export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)"')
fi

# Git setup - derive identity from GitHub token
gh auth setup-git
GH_USER_JSON=$(gh api user -q '{name: .name, login: .login, email: .email, id: .id}')
GH_USER_NAME=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.name // .login')
GH_USER_EMAIL=$(echo "$GH_USER_JSON" | jq -r '.email // "\(.id)+\(.login)@users.noreply.github.com"')
git config --global user.name "$GH_USER_NAME"
git config --global user.email "$GH_USER_EMAIL"

# Clone branch
if [ -n "$REPO_URL" ]; then
    git clone --single-branch --branch "$BRANCH" --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" /job
else
    echo "No REPO_URL provided"
fi

cd /job

# Install npm deps for active skills (native deps need correct Linux arch)
for skill_dir in /job/skills/active/*/; do
    if [ -f "${skill_dir}package.json" ]; then
        echo "Installing skill deps: $(basename "$skill_dir")"
        (cd "$skill_dir" && npm install --omit=dev --no-package-lock)
    fi
done

# Start Chrome if available (installed by browser-tools skill via Puppeteer)
CHROME_PID=""
CHROME_BIN=$(find /home/agent/.cache/puppeteer -name "chrome" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
if [ -n "$CHROME_BIN" ]; then
    $CHROME_BIN --headless --no-sandbox --disable-gpu --remote-debugging-port=9222 2>/dev/null &
    CHROME_PID=$!
    sleep 2
fi

# Setup logs
LOG_DIR="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}"
mkdir -p "${LOG_DIR}"

# 1. Build system prompt from config MD files
SYSTEM_FILES=("SOUL.md" "JOB_AGENT.md")
> /job/.pi/SYSTEM.md
for i in "${!SYSTEM_FILES[@]}"; do
    cat "/job/config/${SYSTEM_FILES[$i]}" >> /job/.pi/SYSTEM.md
    if [ "$i" -lt $((${#SYSTEM_FILES[@]} - 1)) ]; then
        echo -e "\n\n" >> /job/.pi/SYSTEM.md
    fi
done

# Resolve {{datetime}} variable in SYSTEM.md
sed -i "s/{{datetime}}/$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")/g" /job/.pi/SYSTEM.md

# Read job metadata from job.config.json
JOB_CONFIG="/job/logs/${JOB_ID}/job.config.json"
TITLE=$(jq -r '.title // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")
JOB_DESCRIPTION=$(jq -r '.job // empty' "$JOB_CONFIG")

PROMPT="

# Your Job

${JOB_DESCRIPTION}"

LLM_PROVIDER="${LLM_PROVIDER:-anthropic}"

MODEL_FLAGS="--provider $LLM_PROVIDER"
if [ -n "$LLM_MODEL" ]; then
    MODEL_FLAGS="$MODEL_FLAGS --model $LLM_MODEL"
fi

# Generate models.json for custom provider (OpenAI-compatible endpoints like Ollama)
if [ "$LLM_PROVIDER" = "custom" ] && [ -n "$OPENAI_BASE_URL" ]; then
    # If no API key was provided, set a dummy so Pi doesn't send empty auth
    if [ -z "$CUSTOM_API_KEY" ]; then
        export CUSTOM_API_KEY="not-needed"
    fi
    cat > /home/agent/.pi/agent/models.json <<MODELS
{
  "providers": {
    "custom": {
      "baseUrl": "$OPENAI_BASE_URL",
      "api": "openai-completions",
      "apiKey": "CUSTOM_API_KEY",
      "models": [{ "id": "$LLM_MODEL" }]
    }
  }
}
MODELS
fi

# Copy custom models.json to PI's global config if present in repo (overrides generated)
if [ -f "/job/.pi/agent/models.json" ]; then
    mkdir -p /home/agent/.pi/agent
    cp /job/.pi/agent/models.json /home/agent/.pi/agent/models.json
fi

# Run Pi — capture exit code instead of letting set -e kill the script
set +e
pi $MODEL_FLAGS -p "$PROMPT" --session-dir "${LOG_DIR}"
PI_EXIT=$?

# 2. Commit based on outcome
if [ $PI_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    # Pi failed — only commit session logs, not partial code changes
    git reset || true
    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE} (failed)" || true
else
    # Pi succeeded — commit everything
    git add -A
    git add -f "${LOG_DIR}"
    git commit -m "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" || true
fi

git push origin

# Capture log commit SHA, then remove logs so they don't merge into main
LOG_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
git rm -rf "${LOG_DIR}"
git commit -m "done." || true
git push origin
set -e

# Create PR with log permalink (auto-merge handled by GitHub Actions workflow)
REPO_SLUG=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner)
LOG_URL="https://github.com/${REPO_SLUG}/tree/${LOG_SHA}/logs/${JOB_ID}"
gh pr create --title "🤖 Agent Job: ${TITLE}" \
  --body "📋 [View Job Logs](${LOG_URL})"$'\n\n---\n\n'"${JOB_DESCRIPTION}" \
  --base main || true

# Cleanup
if [ -n "$CHROME_PID" ]; then
    kill $CHROME_PID 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Re-raise Pi's failure so the workflow reports it
if [ $PI_EXIT -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "Pi exited with code ${PI_EXIT}"
    exit $PI_EXIT
fi

echo "Done. Job ID: ${JOB_ID}"

Done. Here's what was changed:

**Dockerfiles** (`claude-code-job` and `pi-coding-agent-job`):
- Added `python3`, `python3-pip`, `python3-venv` to the `apt-get install` block

**Entrypoints** (both agents):
- Added a loop after the npm install loop that creates a per-skill virtualenv at `/job/venvs/{skill-name}/` and installs `requirements.txt` into it

This matches the path the `opportunity-analyser` skill expects (`venvs/opportunity-analyser/bin/python3`), so all existing Python calls in the skill scripts will work without modification. The fix applies to any future skill that adds a `requirements.txt` too.
